steps.title: Identify the data pattern: linear or exponential
steps.content:
The points in the scatterplot follow a roughly straight upward path with no curvature, indicating a linear relationship.

This eliminates choices C and D, which are exponential models that would produce curved growth.

steps.title: Determine the slope sign
steps.content:
As $x$ increases from about $1{,}200$ to $4{,}200$, $y$ increases from about $900$ to $4{,}000$. The slope is positive.

Choice B, $y = 582 - 1.2x$, has a negative slope, so it contradicts the upward trend. Only choice A remains.

steps.title: Verify with a point from the graph
steps.content:
Pick the visible data point near $(3{,}500, 3{,}700)$ and substitute into choice A:
$$y = -582 + 1.2(3{,}500) = -582 + 4{,}200 = 3{,}618$$
The predicted value $3{,}618$ closely matches the observed $y \approx 3{,}700$, confirming the fit.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
